title: Local Arcade Repo Audit
auditMode: docs-first local triage; no broad builds or runtime tests executed
notes: protocol-silent-night-prodmod has a .git file and was excluded as a linked worktree rather than counted as a clone directory.
overheat-titan-extraction is represented locally but its origin still points at overheat-titan-extract.
Voxel Realms is not one of the 49 local org clones; it is audited as a current cabinet-only game.

mergeTracks.decision: merge Voxel Realms visual and telemetry techniques into Bok
scopeReduction: ship three biomes first
ship three bosses and Tome arcs first
keep one hub upgrade loop first
make block interaction quest-limited
cap intended runs at 12 to 18 minutes

mergeTracks.decision: merge ideas and content patterns into King's Road, not engines wholesale
scopeReduction: ship one main route plus micro and meso side quests first
defer macro quest chains
use procedural support for encounter texture rather than product identity
treat companion psychology as optional after the main route is playable

mergeTracks.decision: evaluate Pond Warfare engine concepts inside Otter Elite Force identity; use Rivermarsh for biome and mechanics references
scopeReduction: start with one defend-lodge mission
limit resources to two or three readable types
keep specialists to a small named roster first
port concepts to the OEF mission DSL rather than importing Pond's runtime wholesale

mergeTracks.decision: Farm Follies is the active exploratory stacker merge and canonical destination
merge Overheat extraction, heat, cockpit, resource, and feedback ideas into Titan Mech
scopeReduction: reframe as a 5 to 10 minute extraction arena
keep one ore type and one rare isotope first
use one silo and one reactor or pylon objective set
keep combat as readable pressure rather than a full shooter campaign
endless downhill movement
warmth and cocoa survival pressure
one snowman enemy type
one kick action
one photo or flash power-up
touch controls before new content

mergeTracks.decision: merge into one product under jbcom org, not arcade-cabinet

repos.name: aetheria
repos.lastCommit: 2026-04-09T23:43:22-05:00 c4edbd463 docs: standardize documentation for aetheria
repos.statedPurpose: Procedural mobile RPG about a risen skeleton exploring Gothic ruins with archetypes, loot, combat, and ECS simulation.
repos.intendedPlayerExperience: The loop is progression-heavy and mobile-native first, so forcing it into a cabinet cartridge would weaken it.
repos.coreLoop: Choose archetype, explore generated ruins, fight, collect loot, progress character systems.
repos.scope: Large RPG and mobile product; README says web is not a target.
repos.targetPlatform: Expo React Native for iOS and Android; web deprecated.
repos.currentViability: Documented mobile architecture and Maestro direction, but not aligned with one web/Android cabinet runtime.
repos.rationale: The loop is progression-heavy and mobile-native first, so forcing it into a cabinet cartridge would weaken it.
repos.nextAction: Keep outside the cabinet; audit separately as a standalone mobile RPG if it remains strategically important.

repos.name: aethermoor
repos.lastCommit: 2026-01-28T13:18:53-06:00 db0da67 feat: complete Ionic Angular port foundations and onboarding polish
repos.statedPurpose: Mobile-first Ionic Angular and BabylonJS RPG with deterministic generation and large asset/pipeline surface.
repos.intendedPlayerExperience: Standalone RPG/product identity is stronger than a constrained arcade cartridge identity.
repos.coreLoop: Explore an immersive RPG world across web and native with parity to a legacy experience.
repos.scope: Large RPG/application platform with extensive asset, world, and validation scripts.
repos.targetPlatform: Web, iOS, and Android through Capacitor.
repos.currentViability: Strong documentation and many scripts, but scope is far beyond a quick cabinet run.
repos.rationale: Standalone RPG/product identity is stronger than a constrained arcade cartridge identity.
repos.nextAction: Keep standalone; do not import into the central cabinet except maybe as a launcher/deep link later.

repos.intendedPlayerExperience: Manor exploration wants authored pacing, not short cabinet-loop constraints.
repos.coreLoop: Explore a manor-scale authored space with narrative/horror systems.
repos.scope: Large Godot-oriented project with thousands of screenshots/test artifacts.
repos.currentViability: Heavy evidence of work, but engine and content scope make it its own product.
repos.rationale: Manor exploration wants authored pacing, not short cabinet-loop constraints.
repos.nextAction: Treat as standalone; only reuse lessons/assets, not code, for cabinet cartridges.

repos.rationale: A maze escape run can become a strong cabinet cartridge if input and landing identity are simplified.
repos.nextAction: Paper-playtest run length and mobile look controls before considering a one-app cabinet port.
